title: Campus Connect
description: Tutor-student matching platform using an assignment-problem optimization model
long_description: A tutoring platform that models tutor allocation as an assignment problem, using multi-criteria preference vectors to construct a compatibility cost matrix for optimal matching, with schedule feasibility determined through interval-overlap detection
motivation: Built as an undergraduate capstone project to address the lack of a centralized tutoring system for Jamaican university students while exploring algorithmic approaches to preference-based matching
features: Tutor-student matching using weighted preference matrices and similarity scoring
Multi-criteria ranking of tutors based on specified preferences
Interval scheduling system to detect compatible availability windows
Client-server architecture with separated service and controller layers

title: Avocado API
description: Comparative API architecture project implementing REST, GraphQL, and SOAP over the same backend service
long_description: A Spring Boot project implementing identical data operations through REST, GraphQL, and SOAP APIs backed by a shared service layer and PostgreSQL database, enabling direct comparison of API paradigms and their performance characteristics
motivation: Designed to compare API paradigms under identical conditions while evaluating performance and architectural tradeoffs
features: Shared service layer ensuring identical business logic across all APIs
Spring Data JPA repository layer with PostgreSQL persistence
Performance benchmarking using Apache JMeter test plans

title: Caribbean Voices ASR Model
description: Accent-adapted speech recognition system using parameter-efficient fine-tuning of Whisper
long_description: An ASR system adapting Whisper Large-v3 to Caribbean-accented speech using LoRA-based parameter-efficient fine-tuning, with a pipeline for audio preprocessing, seq2seq training, beam-search decoding, and transcription normalization
motivation: Built for the Zindi Caribbean Voices Hackathon to explore efficient adaptation of large speech models to underrepresented Caribbean accents while working within limited compute constraints
features: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning of Whisper Large-v3 using LoRA adapters
Seq2Seq training pipeline using HuggingFace Trainer with mixed precision
Beam search decoding for stable transcription generation
Post-processing pipeline for speech normalization

title: Portfolio
description: Personal portfolio with a terminal-style search and AI chatbot powered by Llama 3.3 using context from my work
long_description: A single-page portfolio built with Vue 3 and FastAPI, featuring a terminal-style interface with intent detection that routes queries between search and an AI chatbot. The chatbot is powered by Llama 3.3 70B via Groq and uses a lightweight RAG-style approach with fuzzy matching to inject relevant context from a curated Q&A dataset.
motivation: I wanted a portfolio that felt more interactive than a typical static site. The terminal interface gave me a way to combine search and conversational interaction to give users more insight into my work and experience.
features: Lightweight RAG pipeline using fuzzy matching to retrieve and inject relevant context
FastAPI backend with modular endpoints for search, chat, project and profile data
Vue 3 frontend with responsive design and SCSS styling

title: IDEATranspiler
description: Transpiler converting legacy VB-style IDEA scripts to Python for modern AML/KYC processing
long_description: A transpiler built with Python and Lark that parses legacy VB-style IDEA scripts using an LALR grammar and generates equivalent Python code, enabling AML/KYC scripts written in legacy IDEA syntax to be processed and analyzed using modern Python.
motivation: Built to automatically convert legacy VB-style IDEA scripts to Python, removing the bottleneck of manual conversions.
features: LALR grammar definition for VB-style IDEA syntax
Parse tree construction and AST traversal using Lark
Python code generation from parsed legacy scripts
Automated conversion of AML/KYC audit scripts to modern Python

title: DKVS
description: Fault-tolerant distributed key-value store implementing the Raft consensus algorithm from scratch in Go
long_description: A distributed key-value store built in Go that implements the Raft consensus algorithm from scratch, ensuring consistent shared state across a 3-node cluster even under node failures. Applicable to problems such as rate limiting, distributed configuration, and feature flag management where multiple servers need to agree on shared values reliably.
motivation: Applications running across multiple servers have no reliable way to share state without risking inconsistency when nodes fail. This solves the problem at the consensus layer, using the Raft algorithm.
features: Raft leader election with randomized timeouts to prevent split votes
Log replication with majority quorum commit guarantee
Automatic failover when leader crashes with sub-300ms recovery
Rejoining nodes automatically catch up via leader heartbeats
Supports SET, DELETE, INCREMENT, and COMPARE_AND_SET operations
